transform_insights.name: Loop Unrolling (factor 4)
transform_insights.description: Replicates the loop body 4 times per iteration, reducing the loop overhead (branch, compare, increment) by 4x. Adds an epilog loop to handle remaining iterations when N is not a multiple of 4.

why_helped.explanation: 4x fewer loop-back branches means the branch predictor has 4x fewer opportunities to mispredict the loop exit.
With fewer branches stalling the pipeline, the CPU's out-of-order engine keeps more execution units busy processing the 4 loads and adds per iteration.
45% speedup from eliminated branch overhead and better instruction-level parallelism across the 4 unrolled copies.

why_hurt.explanation: Although total dynamic instructions decreased (fewer loop overhead instructions), the static code size increased. The epilog loop adds extra instructions for cleanup.

trade_offs: Instruction cache pressure: 4x code duplication increases I-cache footprint. With large loop bodies, this could cause I-cache misses that negate the branch reduction benefit.
Epilog overhead: For small N (< 4), the epilog loop does all the work and the main loop never executes — no benefit from unrolling.
Register pressure: 4 simultaneous partial sums may spill registers on architectures with few GPRs, though x86-64 has enough.
